panels.title: Fighting games — things to investigate
panels.content:
## Player data as mirrored structs

Fighting games almost always store P1 and P2 data in the same structure, at a fixed byte offset from each other. Once you find the address for P1 health, P2 health is likely the same distance away as P1 max HP is from P1 current HP — the struct repeats. This pattern often extends to the super meter, round wins, and character ID.

## Round timer

The displayed round timer and its raw value in RAM are frequently the same number in the same units. Searching for the value you see on screen is a direct approach.

## Character unlock flags

Character unlock states are almost always packed into a bitmask. Unlocking one character will cause a single bit to flip in a byte, not a standalone value to increment from 0 to 1.

## Clears per character

Arcade mode completion is typically tracked per character. The flag for whether a specific character has cleared arcade mode may be a bit in that character's data block rather than at a global address.

## Short-lived values

Some values — damage dealt on the last hit, the critical hit flag, the perfect round flag — exist only for a very short window. Pause-scanning immediately after the event occurs is one way to catch them.

panels.title: Protection
panels.infobox: Identify these before writing achievement logic.

entry_Protection.name: Demo mode
entry_Protection.tNote: Check for a specific value that is set or cleared only on the main menu or when demo mode initiates.

entry_Protection.name: Save protection
entry_Protection.tNote: Find values that match a loading state or last loaded in-game state so that you can confirm the player is in-game for at least one frame by checking Delta = in-game.

entry_Protection.name: Cheat protection
entry_Protection.tNote: Investigate old guides or walkthroughs for possible cheats. If they offer an advantage, block them. Look for on/off flags, unnatural resource increases during a menu or pause scenario.

entry_Protection.name: Infinite health cheat
entry_Protection.tNote: If this exists, damageless and perfect-round achievements must require it to be off.

entry_Protection.name: Infinite super meter cheat
entry_Protection.tNote: If this exists, super-based challenge achievements must require it to be off.

entry_Protection.name: All characters cheat
entry_Protection.tNote: If this exists, character unlock achievements must require it to be off.

entry_Protection.name: CPU behavior modifier
entry_Protection.tNote: Cheats that change CPU difficulty or behavior. Investigate whether any affect achievement validity.

entry_Protection.name: Training mode flag
entry_Protection.tNote: Training mode may have infinite health or meter enabled by default. Investigate what values change when entering training.

entry_Protection.name: 2-player human input
entry_Protection.tNote: A second human player changes match dynamics. Investigate whether solo achievements need to exclude this state.

panels.title: Progression
panels.infobox: Values that track forward movement through arcade mode, story, and character unlocks.

entry_Progression.name: Game mode ID
entry_Progression.tNote: What separates arcade, story, vs CPU, vs player, and training modes.

entry_Progression.name: Arcade stage (opponent index)
entry_Progression.tNote: Which opponent in the arcade ladder is being faced.

entry_Progression.name: Round state / phase
entry_Progression.tNote: Pre-fight intro, active round, round over, and match over states.

entry_Progression.name: Round number
entry_Progression.tNote: Which round of the current match is being played.

entry_Progression.name: Match outcome flag
entry_Progression.tNote: Whether the match ended in a win, loss, draw, or timeout.

entry_Progression.name: Arcade mode cleared flag
entry_Progression.tNote: A value set when the full arcade ladder is completed with any character.

entry_Progression.name: Story mode chapter
entry_Progression.tNote: Which chapter of story mode is active, if the game has a story mode.

entry_Progression.name: Character unlock flags
entry_Progression.tNote: Which characters have been unlocked. Investigate whether this is a bitmask or individual flags.

entry_Progression.name: Ending seen — char 1
entry_Progression.tNote: Whether character 1's arcade ending has been viewed.

entry_Progression.name: Ending seen — char 2
entry_Progression.tNote: Same for character 2. Continue for each character.

entry_Progression.name: Clears per character
entry_Progression.tNote: How many times each character has cleared arcade mode, if tracked separately.

entry_Progression.name: Game completion flag
entry_Progression.tNote: A value set when a meaningful completion state is reached — all characters cleared, all endings seen, or similar.

panels.title: Collections
panels.infobox: Things unlocked that persist across sessions.

entry_Collection.name: Character unlock flags
entry_Collection.tNote: All character unlock states. Investigate whether packed into a bitmask or individual addresses.

entry_Collection.name: Character unlock count
entry_Collection.tNote: A total count of characters available, if this exists alongside the flags.

entry_Collection.name: Costume / palette unlocks
entry_Collection.tNote: Alternate costumes or color palettes that have been unlocked.

entry_Collection.name: Gallery content flags
entry_Collection.tNote: Artwork, music, or movies unlocked in a gallery mode.

entry_Collection.name: Sound test unlock flags
entry_Collection.tNote: Which background music tracks are available in a sound test, if one exists.

entry_Collection.name: Story completion — char 1
entry_Collection.tNote: Whether character 1's story has been completed.

entry_Collection.name: Story completion — char 2
entry_Collection.tNote: Same for character 2. Continue for each character.

entry_Collection.name: Arcade clear — char 1
entry_Collection.tNote: Whether character 1 has cleared arcade mode.

entry_Collection.name: Arcade clear — char 2
entry_Collection.tNote: Same for character 2. Continue for each character.

entry_Collection.name: Survival mode record
entry_Collection.tNote: Best score or win count in a survival mode, if one exists.

entry_Collection.name: In-game currency
entry_Collection.tNote: Spendable currency for unlocking content, if the game has this system.

panels.title: Challenges
panels.infobox: Values for achievements with pass/fail conditions.

entry_Challenge.name: P1 health (current)
entry_Challenge.tNote: Current health of player 1.

entry_Challenge.name: P1 health (max)
entry_Challenge.tNote: Maximum health of player 1. Useful for ratio and threshold logic.

entry_Challenge.name: P2 health (current)
entry_Challenge.tNote: Current health of player 2. The offset from P1 current reveals the struct size.

entry_Challenge.name: P2 health (max)
entry_Challenge.tNote: Maximum health of player 2.

entry_Challenge.name: Round timer
entry_Challenge.tNote: The countdown timer for the current round. The displayed value and RAM value are frequently the same number.

entry_Challenge.name: P1 round wins
entry_Challenge.tNote: Wins accumulated by P1 in the current match.

entry_Challenge.name: P2 round wins
entry_Challenge.tNote: Wins accumulated by P2. May be adjacent to P1 wins.

entry_Challenge.name: P1 super / special meter
entry_Challenge.tNote: P1's current special meter value.

entry_Challenge.name: P2 super / special meter
entry_Challenge.tNote: P2's meter. May follow the same struct offset as health.

entry_Challenge.name: Combo counter
entry_Challenge.tNote: Current hit count in an active combo string.

entry_Challenge.name: P1 character ID
entry_Challenge.tNote: Which character P1 selected.

entry_Challenge.name: P2 character ID
entry_Challenge.tNote: Which character P2 selected.

entry_Challenge.name: Stage / arena ID
entry_Challenge.tNote: Which stage is being fought on.

entry_Challenge.name: Perfect round flag
entry_Challenge.tNote: A value that is set when a round is won with full health. May not exist as a native flag — may need to be derived from HP.

entry_Challenge.name: Timeout flag
entry_Challenge.tNote: A value that is set when time expires before a KO.

entry_Challenge.name: Damage dealt (last hit)
entry_Challenge.tNote: The value of the most recent attack. A short-lived value.

entry_Challenge.name: Clears per character — char 1
entry_Challenge.tNote: How many times character 1 has cleared arcade mode.

entry_Challenge.name: Clears per character — char 2
entry_Challenge.tNote: Same for character 2.

entry_Challenge.name: Clears per character — char 3
entry_Challenge.tNote: Continue for each playable character.

entry_Challenge.name: Difficulty setting (current)
entry_Challenge.tNote: The active difficulty during the match. Scope anchor for difficulty-gated challenges.
